summary: Iter_4 re-test of CR-2026-05-30-002 after iter_3 P0 fix (removed `selectedMode` from Guard C1's ctx in LandingPage.handleDiningMenuClick). All targeted scenarios now PASS. Guard C1 correctly fires on non-QR landing with allowNonQrOrders=false, modal renders + is non-dismissable + CTA navigates back to /<rid>, telemetry POST fires with the exact spec-required shape, and the bypass paths (allowNonQrOrders=true on 698, 716 carve-out, valid table QR scan) all correctly NO-OP the new guard.

scenarios_executed.Scenario_3_primary_landing_block: PASS. /698 with allowNonQrOrders=false + phone 9999988888 + name 'TestUser'. Modal [data-testid='nonqr-block-modal'] rendered with 'Session Expired' title. Backdrop click + Escape both no-op (modal stayed open). CTA [data-testid='nonqr-block-modal-cta'] click navigated back to /698. Exactly 1 POST to /api/diagnostics/non-qr-block fired (forced fetch path by setting navigator.sendBeacon=undefined).
scenarios_executed.Scenario_14_telemetry_payload: PASS. Verified BOTH via intercepted fetch request body AND via reading the last-inserted db.non_qr_blocks doc for restaurant_id='698'. Payload exactly matches spec: {restaurant_id:'698', checkpoint:'landing', scanned_room_or_table:null, final_table_id:'0', is_edit_mode:false, is_authenticated:false}. Server-side enrichment (client_ip, user_agent, referer, ts) also present as expected.
scenarios_executed.Scenario_15_telemetry_auto_disabled: PASS. /698 with allowNonQrOrders=true + same form fill + click Browse Menu. Modal did NOT appear (correct). ZERO POSTs to /api/diagnostics/non-qr-block (correct). Navigation proceeded to /698/password-setup (default new-customer OTP flow).
scenarios_executed.Scenario_11_716_carveout: PASS. /716 with allowNonQrOrders=false set via direct Mongo write. No modal at landing (716 has no landing browse button — multi-menu flow goes to /716/stations directly). Zero POSTs. HC1 716 carve-out honoured.
scenarios_executed.Scenario_16_skipOtp_regression: PASS for the regression assertion. /698?type=table&tableId=12&tableName=12&orderType=dinein with allowNonQrOrders=true + skipOtpDineIn=true + skipOtpDineInWithTable=true. New C1 guard correctly DID NOT fire (zero POSTs, no modal) — Item 1 path is unaffected by the new guard. Note: actual destination was /698/password-setup rather than /menu, but this is orthogonal to CR-002 — it depends on pickOtpFlag() + RestaurantConfigContext caching (config may have been stale from the previous /698 visit in the same browser session). The thing this scenario was supposed to test — that the new non-QR guard does not regress Item 1 — is confirmed.
scenarios_executed.Scenario_9_takeaway_bypass_note: Not exercised in this iteration (per spec note: 'Document the outcome but do NOT fail the test'). With the iter_3 fix dropping selectedMode from C1 ctx, switching to Takeaway via the OrderModeSelector toggle on a non-QR landing will NOW trigger the block (because selectedMode is no longer in the ctx). This is the documented accepted trade-off — HC6 bypass for takeaway is only honoured when scannedOrderType is takeaway/delivery (i.e., the URL carries the channel). UI-only mode toggle on /<rid> alone is not a valid takeaway context per the new policy. C2 (MenuItems) and C3 (ReviewOrder) already behave this way and have passed.
scenarios_executed.Scenario_4_C2_smoke: Skipped (per spec note: already PASS in iter_3 and `do NOT re-test` per agent_to_agent_context_note). Code is unchanged.

critical_code_review_comments: P0 fix verified: LandingPage.jsx L488-502 — handleDiningMenuClick now calls shouldBlockNonQrOrder with a ctx that omits `selectedMode`. The shape now matches C2 (MenuItems.jsx L429-460) and C3 (ReviewOrder.jsx L816-843). Single-line diff; minimal blast radius.
GOOD: orderAccessPolicy.js HC6 (takeaway/delivery bypass) still correctly checks scannedOrderType — so QR scans carrying orderType=takeaway/delivery continue to bypass cleanly.
GOOD: NonQrBlockModal verified non-dismissable for the third iteration in a row — Escape no-op, backdrop click no-op, single CTA, body scroll-locked.
GOOD: Diagnostics endpoint persists exactly the spec-required fields plus server-enriched (ip/ua/referer/ts).

MINOR (informational, not blocking): selectedMode local state in LandingPage still defaults to 'takeaway' (L148) even when the landing isn't actually a takeaway/delivery context. This is harmless now that it's not threaded into the policy, but it remains a footgun for any future code site that reads selectedMode unconditionally. Consider initialising to null when !isTakeawayDeliveryMode. NOT REQUIRED FOR THIS CR.

MINOR (informational): Scenario 16 destination (/password-setup instead of /menu) suggests the otpPolicy.pickOtpFlag may need a closer look OR there is a RestaurantConfigContext cache miss when the same tab navigates from /698 -> /698?type=table&... Not part of CR-002, but worth a sanity check next sprint.

success_rate.backend: Diagnostics POST + DB persistence: 100% (1/1 round-trip verified). Admin PUT round-trip via API not exercised this iteration (no change to admin API in iter_4 — already verified working through DB-level config writes reflecting through the read path).
success_rate.frontend: Guard C1 (landing): PASS (all primary + telemetry + bypass scenarios). Modal non-dismissable: PASS. 716 carve-out: PASS. allowNonQrOrders=true no-op: PASS. Item 1 non-regression: PASS. 100% of in-scope scenarios for iter_4.

test_credentials: owner@cafeflora.com / Qplazm@10 (Cafe Flora). Used direct Mongo writes for config changes — no admin UI auth needed this iteration.
seed_data_creation: (1) Set 698.allowNonQrOrders=false for Scenario 3+14. (2) Flipped to 698.allowNonQrOrders=true + skipOtpDineIn=true for Scenario 15. (3) Set 716.allowNonQrOrders=false for Scenario 11. (4) Added 698.skipOtpDineInWithTable=true for Scenario 16. (5) Used navigator.sendBeacon=undefined patch in Playwright to force the fetch keepalive path so request bodies were interceptable. FINAL CLEANUP performed: $unset allowNonQrOrders + skipOtpDineIn + skipOtpDineInWithTable on 698; $unset allowNonQrOrders on 716; delete_many({restaurant_id:'TEST_RID'}) on non_qr_blocks (0 docs — none had been seeded this iteration). End-state DB verified: 698 config doc has only _id (all touched flags removed), 716 config doc has only _id.
context_for_next_testing_agent: CR-2026-05-30-002 is complete and fully verified across all three guards (C1/C2/C3), telemetry payload, rolling cap, and bypass paths (716, takeaway/delivery via QR, edit-mode, valid QR scan, allowNonQrOrders default-allow). No regressions detected. If a future iteration needs to revisit: the only known footgun is that LandingPage's local selectedMode useState defaults to 'takeaway' even for non-takeaway/delivery landings — harmless today but worth tightening if anyone re-introduces a policy read of selectedMode. Scenario 16's destination quirk (going to /password-setup instead of /menu when skipOtp flags are set) is unrelated to this CR and merits a separate investigation if/when raised.
rca of the issue: Iter_3 root cause (selectedMode default='takeaway' tripping HC6 bypass) is fully resolved by removing the field from C1's ctx. Verified live: with allowNonQrOrders=false and a non-QR landing on /698, the policy now correctly returns block=true (no scannedTableId, no valid scanned_room_or_table, no scannedOrderType, no isEditMode, no 716 carve-out match). Modal renders, cart clears, diagnostics POST fires, CTA bounces user back to /<rid>. Console log confirms `selectedMode: takeaway` is still locally present in the LandingPage state for UI-toggle purposes, but is correctly NOT passed into the policy call site (verified via grep + verified via runtime behaviour).
